message:
feat(runtime): install RuntimeHandle on worker threads and decouple thread callbacks from block_on

Refactor the runtime initialization to make RuntimeHandle available on
worker threads via the same thread-local mechanism used by block_on,
enabling Runtime::current_handle() to work from spawned tasks.

Key changes:

- RuntimeInner::new() now returns (Self, Vec<ThreeLaneWorker>) instead of
  spawning workers internally. This allows Runtime::new_from_parts() to
  Arc-wrap the inner first, then pass &Arc<Self> to spawn_worker_threads.

- spawn_worker_threads() takes &Arc<RuntimeInner> instead of &RuntimeConfig
  + &mut ThreeLaneScheduler. Each worker thread now installs a
  ScopedRuntimeHandle guard before entering the run loop, making
  Runtime::current_handle() available from any task polled on that worker.

- Worker thread handles are stored into the existing worker_threads Mutex
  after spawning, rather than returned as a Vec.

- block_on() no longer fires on_thread_start/on_thread_stop callbacks.
  These callbacks are for worker threads, not the calling thread. The
  block_on caller is an external thread that should not receive worker
  lifecycle events. This prevents double-firing when the caller is also
  a thread that received callbacks during runtime construction.

Add tests:
- current_handle_available_inside_spawned_task: verifies a spawned task
  can discover the runtime handle and spawn a nested task
- thread_callbacks_do_not_fire_for_block_on_caller: verifies on_thread_start
  and on_thread_stop fire exactly once (for the worker), not for block_on

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
